Welcome back to The Varsity, my twice-weekly private email on the
sports business cinematic universe. I am coming to you today from the Big E=
asy, where the security presence is noticeably higher than in previous year=
s. And it=E2=80=99s not because Marchand has moved his annual poetry slam c=
ompetition to the convention center this year. (All right, Andrew, I sent y=
ou to Caf=C3=A9 du Monde two hours ago. The line can=E2=80=99t be that long=
! And tell them not to skimp on the powdered sugar this time!)

=C2=A0

Rao=E2=80=99s, the beloved and impossible-to-get-into East Harlem restauran=
t (which has since opened in L.A. and Vegas and spawned a remarkable C.P.G.=
 line), has opened a popup in New Orleans for Super Bowl week. I was there =
last night putting on the nosebag, and it was a scene=E2=80=94not just beca=
use Mad Dog was sitting at the next table with his agent, Sandy Montag. As =
dessert was served=E2=80=94bread pudding and beignets, if you must know=E2=
=80=94our
hosts convinced Run DMC=E2=80=99s Darryl =E2=80=9CDMC=E2=80=9D McDaniels to=

er preseason contest and adding an 18th regular season game. Murmurs emanat=
ing from New Orleans suggest that the public negotiations have begun.

Quaint were the days when the NFL played a 16-game schedule with a monthlon=
g
preseason slate. As I=E2=80=99ve been documenting over the past several mon=
ths, after all, the league seems to be fantasizing out loud about its own e=
xpanding manifest destiny=E2=80=94in particular, eliminating yet another pr=
eseason game from its schedule to accommodate an 18th regular season weeken=
d of matchups.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

Commissioner Roger Goodell justified the previous extension to 17 games by
philosophizing that the players weren=E2=80=99t really adding another game =
to their calendar, so much as they were simply swapping a meaningless conte=
st for a consequential one. It was a cute line, but was also pure sophistry=
=2E As everyone knows, it=E2=80=99s standa=
rd practice for starters and vetera=
ns to limit their reps during the preseason. Adding a new regular season ga=
me will always carry health risks, even if it produces a financial windfall=
, and would need the approval of the NFL
Players Association.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

Anyway, among the many storylines emanating from Super Bowl week=E2=80=94ab=
surd mania about Chiefs favoritism, the depressing elimination of the =E2=
=80=9CEnd Racism=E2=80=9D endzone signage, concerns about the evolving role=
 of sports betting=E2=80=94has been a vibe shift regarding the 18th game. O=
n Monday, Goodell tried to temper some of the optimism during his press con=
ference, noting that the league hasn=E2=80=99t
had any =E2=80=9Cformal negotiating sessions about an extension.=E2=80=9D H=
e also emphasized that any change to the schedule would be negotiated as pa=
rt of the collective bargaining agreement, which runs to 2030=E2=80=94putti=
ng the decision off a handful of years to perhaps let some air out of the t=
rial balloon. Mark Shapiro, the president of Endeavor and TKO, applauded Go=
odell=E2=80=99s change in tone when we caught up this week. In particular, =
he was grateful that the commish was managing the time frame more
carefully. (Disclosure: WME, Shapiro=E2=80=99s talent agency, represents Pu=
ck, but not me personally.)

=C2=A0

After Goodell threw some cold water on the concept, NFLPA executive directo=
r Lloyd Howell Jr. added a few bucketfuls of his own. =E2=80=9CNo one wants=
 to play an 18th game,=E2=80=9D he said during his own press conference lat=
er in the week. =E2=80=9CNo one. Seventeen games is already, for many of th=
e guys, too
long.=E2=80=9D=C2=A0

=C2=A0

Howell, a former Booz Allen executive and Harvard M.B.A. who sits on the bo=
ard of GE HealthCare, is a talented negotiator. He knows how much NFL owner=
s want to add an 18th game, which would create a new media-rights package t=
hat could potentially generate another $1 billion in annual revenue. He als=
o knows that the NFL is likely to exercise an out in its current broadcast =
TV contracts after
the 2029 season=E2=80=94a move that would coincide with the beginning of C.=
B.A. negotiations. The 18th game will be his ultimate bargaining chip.=C2=
=A0

=C2=A0

In their respective press conferences, Goodell and Howell offered a preview=
 of how those negotiations might play out. On Monday, Goodell touted the vi=
rtues of international expansion and noted that concussions were at an hist=
orically low level this year, citing rule changes and
equipment improvements. Howell remarked that he=E2=80=99d seen data indicat=
ing that an added game would result in more injuries. He also mentioned tha=
t international travel would exacerbate player health issues. Let the games=
 begin=E2=80=A6=C2=A0

n-bottom:0px;">Quaint were the days when the NFL played a 16-game schedule =
with a monthlong
preseason slate. As I=E2=80=99ve been documenting over the past several mon=
ths, after all, the league seems to be fantasizing out loud about its own e=
xpanding manifest destiny=E2=80=94in particular, eliminating yet another pr=
eseason game from its schedule to accommodate an <em>18th </em>regular seas=
on weekend of matchups.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=
=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Commissioner <strong>Rog=
er Goodell</strong> justified the previous extension to 17 games by
philosophizing that the players weren=E2=80=99t <em>really </em>adding <em>=
another</em> game to their calendar, so much as they were simply swapping a=
 meaningless contest for a consequential one. It was a cute line, but was a=
lso pure sophistry. As everyone knows, it=E2=80=99s standard practice for s=
tarters and veterans to limit their reps during the preseason. Adding a new=
 regular season game will always carry health risks, even if it produces a =
financial windfall, and would need the approval of the NFL
Players Association.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=
=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Anyway, among the many stor=
ylines emanating from Super Bowl week=E2=80=94absurd mania about Chiefs fav=
oritism, the depressing elimination of the =E2=80=9CEnd Racism=E2=80=9D end=
zone signage, concerns about the evolving role of sports betting=E2=80=94ha=
s been a vibe shift regarding the 18th game. On Monday, Goodell tried to te=
mper some of the optimism during his press conference, noting that the leag=
ue hasn=E2=80=99t
had any =E2=80=9Cformal negotiating sessions about an extension.=E2=80=9D H=
e also emphasized that any change to the schedule would be negotiated as pa=
rt of the collective bargaining agreement, which runs to 2030=E2=80=94putti=
ng the decision off a handful of years to perhaps let some air out of the t=
rial balloon. <strong>Mark Shapiro</strong>, the president of Endeavor and =
TKO, applauded Goodell=E2=80=99s change in tone when we caught up this week=
=2E In particular, he was grateful that=
 the commish was managing the time fra=
me more
carefully. (Disclosure: WME, Shapiro=E2=80=99s talent agency, represents Pu=
ck, but not me personally.)</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=
=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">After Goodell threw some co=
ld water on the concept, NFLPA executive director <strong>Lloyd Howell Jr.<=
/strong> added a few bucketfuls of his own. =E2=80=9CNo one wants to play a=
n 18th game,=E2=80=9D he said during his own press conference later in the =
week. =E2=80=9CNo one. Seventeen games is already, for many of the guys, to=
o
long.=E2=80=9D=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p>=
<p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Howell, a former Booz Allen execut=
ive and Harvard M.B.A. who sits on the board of GE HealthCare, is a talente=
d negotiator. He knows how much NFL owners want to add an 18th game, which =
would create a new media-rights package that could potentially generate ano=
ther $1 billion in annual revenue. He also knows that the NFL is likely to =
exercise an out in its current broadcast TV contracts after
the 2029 season=E2=80=94a move that would coincide with the beginning of C.=
B.A. negotiations. The 18th game will be his ultimate bargaining chip.=C2=
=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margi=
n:0;">In their respective press conferences, Goodell and Howell offered a p=
review of how those negotiations might play out. On Monday, Goodell touted =
the virtues of international expansion and noted that concussions were at a=
n historically low level this year, citing rule changes and
equipment improvements. Howell remarked that he=E2=80=99d seen data indicat=
ing that an added game would result in more injuries. He also mentioned tha=
t international travel would exacerbate player health issues. Let the games=
 begin=E2=80=A6=C2=A0</p></div>
